title: Patent mapping
abstract: System and method permit patent mapping. A method may comprise maintaining a database of patent portfolios and a database of patents with each patent stored in the database of patents associated with one or more patent portfolios stored in the database of patent portfolios. A target subject matter to be mapped is identified and a search query associated with the target subject matter is received. Search results are generated to define a first patent portfolio in the database with the search results including one or more patent claims associated with the search query. The one or more patent claims are mapped to a patent concept.
claims: 1. A computer implemented method of managing prior art comprising: identifying a source patent matter in a patent portfolio; obtaining a document associated with the source patent matter; assigning an applicable prior art status value to the document based on an evaluation of document data of the document; classifying the document as a prior art reference based on the applicable prior art status value; converting the patent portfolio into a first prior art portfolio; adding an association between the prior art reference and the first prior art portfolio; recording an association date for the association between the prior art reference and the source patent matter of the first prior art portfolio, wherein the association date is a date when the prior art reference was first cited in the source patent matter of the first prior art portfolio; determining a citation chain between the source patent matter and a second patent matter, wherein the citation chain identifies successive levels of prior art reference flow from the source patent matter through one or more intermediate prior art portfolios to the second patent matter; causing the prior art reference to automatically flow to a second prior art portfolio, the second prior art portfolio including at least one common patent matter with the first prior art portfolio; determining an application status for a patent matter; causing the prior art reference to flow to the patent matter based on the application status; tracking a flow level indicator for each prior art portfolio in the citation chain, wherein the flow level indicator designates a successive generation level of prior art reference flow from the source patent matter, wherein a flow level of zero is assigned to the source patent matter, a flow level of one is assigned to prior art references flowing to other patent matters in the first prior art portfolio, and a flow level of two or higher is assigned to prior art references flowing to patent matters in the second prior art portfolio; and providing, in a display, a flow level indicator generated by calculating a number of prior art portfolios in the citation chain based on the tracked flow level indicators to determine relevancy of the prior art reference to the patent matter, wherein relevancy is inversely proportional to the calculated number of portfolios in the citation chain, the association date, and a mapping status indicator selected at least in part based on a document type of the prior art reference.
